title: Project Report: Retail Sales Dashboard — Power BI Analyst
author: Gagan Pasupuleti
summary: Power BI report from Excel/CSV: Power Query cleanup, star schema, DAX measures, published to Service.
objectives: Import and transform with Power Query
source_file: CodeQuest project specification (official docs + industry patterns)

chapters.title: Project Overview
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Project Overview** *(Level: Beginner)*

**Retail Sales Dashboard** is a beginner-level end-to-end project for Power BI Analyst. Duration: 2 weeks. Power BI report from Excel/CSV: Power Query cleanup, star schema, DAX measures, published to Service. Tools: Power BI Desktop, Power BI Service.

Code Reference:
```text
Objective: Import and transform with Power Query
Objective: Build star schema model
Objective: Create 6 visuals + slicers
Objective: Publish and share
```
What it shows: Lists measurable outcomes before you start building.

### What it actually is
A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.

### When to use it
At project kickoff.

### Where to use it
Power BI Analyst portfolios and CodeQuest project submissions.

### Real use example
A candidate lists this project on a Power BI Analyst resume with a demo link.
key_takeaways: A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.
A candidate lists this project on a Power BI Analyst resume with a demo link.

chapters.title: Phase: Import
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Import** *(Level: Beginner)*

Connect to sales data. Complete these tasks: Remove errors; Change types. Deliverable for this phase: Clean query.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] Remove errors
- [ ] Change types
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### What it actually is
Phase Import is one step in the full project lifecycle.

### When to use it
During week 1 of the project timeline.

### Where to use it
Power BI Analyst agile sprints and coursework milestones.

### Real use example
Team demo shows the Clean query to the teacher.

chapters.title: Phase: DAX
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: DAX** *(Level: Beginner)*

Revenue, YoY, margin measures. Complete these tasks: CALCULATE; TOTALYTD. Deliverable for this phase: Measure list doc.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] CALCULATE
- [ ] TOTALYTD
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### What it actually is
Phase DAX is one step in the full project lifecycle.

### When to use it
During week 3 of the project timeline.

### Where to use it
Power BI Analyst agile sprints and coursework milestones.


chapters.title: Final Deliverables and Review
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Final Deliverables and Review** *(Level: Beginner)*

Submit all final deliverables: PBIX file; Published report link; DAX measure documentation. Skills demonstrated: Power Query, DAX, Modeling. Prepare a 5-minute walkthrough video and README for CodeQuest teacher marking.

Code Reference:
```text
Deliverable: PBIX file
Deliverable: Published report link
Deliverable: DAX measure documentation
```
What it shows: Final checklist ensures nothing is missing at submission.

### What it actually is
Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.

### When to use it
Before deadline and teacher review.

### Where to use it
CodeQuest project gallery and interviews.

### Real use example
A student submits PBIX file and passes the rubric.
key_takeaways: Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.
